task_completion.description: Verify that a Computed Variable named 'ln_count' was created with the correct formula.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Jamovi spreadsheet view, there should be a new column named 'ln_count'. The formula bar or variable setup window should show the formula 'LN(count + 1)' applied to this column.

task_completion.description: Verify that a One-Way ANOVA is set up with the correct dependent and grouping variables.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Jamovi analysis panel should show 'One-Way ANOVA' with 'ln_count' placed in the 'Dependent Variables' box and 'spray' placed in the 'Grouping Variable' box.

task_completion.description: Verify that Tukey post-hoc tests were included in the ANOVA analysis.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Jamovi results panel should display a 'Post Hoc Tests' table (specifically noting Tukey) comparing the different spray groups.

task_completion.description: Verify that the Jamovi project was saved to the specified path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Jamovi window title bar should display 'InsectSprays_Transformed.omv', or a file manager/terminal window should show the file 'InsectSprays_Transformed.omv' existing in the '/home/ga/Documents/Jamovi/' directory.

task_completion.description: Verify that the results were documented correctly in anova_report.txt.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or terminal output showing the contents of '/home/ga/Documents/Jamovi/anova_report.txt'. It must contain exactly three lines: an F-statistic around 46.006, a p-value (e.g., < 0.001), and the number 3 (representing the count of sprays significantly different from Spray A).

integrity.description: Ensure the agent actually used the Jamovi GUI to perform the analysis.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots must show the Jamovi application open with the dataset loaded, the computed variable configured, and the ANOVA results rendered in the right-hand panel. The agent should not bypass Jamovi by using Python/R scripts in the terminal.

integrity.description: Ensure the agent derived the results from the software rather than hallucinating or copying from external knowledge.
integrity.visual_evidence: The values entered into 'anova_report.txt' (F-statistic ~46.006, p < 0.001, and 3 significant differences) must match the output visible in the Jamovi results panel in previous screenshots.

integrity.description: Ensure the report file was genuinely created by the agent.
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent should be seen using a text editor (like nano, gedit) or terminal commands (like echo or cat) to write the three lines into 'anova_report.txt'.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The correct F-statistic for the One-Way ANOVA on the log-transformed data (LN(count + 1)) is approximately 46.006 (acceptable tolerance is +/- 0.5). The p-value is < 0.001. The Tukey post-hoc test results should show exactly 3 sprays (C, D, and E) are significantly different from Spray A (p < 0.05). The final text report must contain these three values.
